root_text.sn38.12:1.6: “Atthi panāvuso, maggo atthi paṭipadā, etesaṁ upādānānaṁ pahānāyā”ti?
root_text.sn38.12:1.7: “Atthi kho, āvuso, maggo atthi paṭipadā, etesaṁ upādānānaṁ pahānāyā”ti.
root_text.sn38.12:1.8: “Katamo panāvuso, maggo katamā paṭipadā, etesaṁ upādānānaṁ pahānāyā”ti?
root_text.sn38.12:1.9: “Ayameva kho, āvuso, ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo, etesaṁ upādānānaṁ pahānāya, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.sn38.12:1.11: Ayaṁ kho, āvuso, maggo ayaṁ paṭipadā, etesaṁ upādānānaṁ pahānāyā”ti.
root_text.sn38.12:1.12: “Bhaddako, āvuso, maggo bhaddikā paṭipadā, etesaṁ upādānānaṁ pahānāya.

translation_text.sn38.12:1.1: “Reverend Sāriputta, they speak of this thing called ‘grasping’.
translation_text.sn38.12:1.3: “Reverend, there are these four kinds of grasping.
translation_text.sn38.12:1.4: Grasping at sensual pleasures, views, precepts and observances, and theories of a self.
translation_text.sn38.12:1.5: These are the four kinds of grasping.”
translation_text.sn38.12:1.6: “But, reverend, is there a path and a practice for completely understanding these four kinds of grasping?”
